 {"id":"caam-7vg","title":"Implement TUI backup action","description":"# Task: Implement TUI backup action

## Context
Wire up the backup functionality to the TUI using the new text input dialog component.

## Implementation

### Trigger
- Press 'b' or 'B' when viewing a provider's profile list
- Only available when auth files exist for that provider

### Flow
1. User presses 'b' on a provider
2. Check if auth files exist for provider
   - If not: show error message "No auth files found for <provider>"
   - If yes: continue
3. Open text input dialog
   - Title: "Backup Current Auth"
   - Prompt: "Enter profile name (e.g., email address):"
   - Placeholder: "user@example.com"
4. User enters profile name, presses Enter
5. Validate input:
   - Not empty
   - No forbidden characters (/, \, etc.)
   - Warn if profile already exists (overwrite?)
6. Call vault.Backup(provider, profileName)
7. Handle result:
   - Success: show message "Backed up <provider> auth to '<profile>'"
   - Error: show error message
8. Refresh profile list

### Overwrite Confirmation
If profile already exists, show confirmation dialog:
- "Profile '<name>' already exists. Overwrite? (y/n)"
- Or use the dialog with Yes/No buttons

### Key Bindings
- Add 'b' to the help text
- Document in keyboard shortcuts section

## Code Changes

### internal/tui/update.go
```go
case "b", "B":
    if m.canBackup() {
        return m.showBackupDialog()
    }
```

### internal/tui/model.go
Add method:
```go
func (m Model) showBackupDialog() (Model, tea.Cmd) {
    // Open dialog for backup
}

func (m Model) executeBackup(profileName string) tea.Cmd {
    return func() tea.Msg {
        err := m.vault.Backup(m.selectedProvider, profileName)
        // Return result message
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 'b' key triggers backup dialog when on provider
- [ ] Dialog prompts for profile name
- [ ] Validation prevents empty/invalid names
- [ ] Existing profile triggers overwrite confirmation
- [ ] Successful backup shows confirmation message
- [ ] Failed backup shows error message
- [ ] Profile list refreshes after backup
- [ ] Help text updated with new keybinding

- Depends on: caam-i5i (text input dialog component)

 {"id":"caam-82c","title":"Add force-unlock command","description":"## Task
Add 'caam unlock' command to forcibly remove locks.

## Usage
```bash
caam unlock codex work-1          # Unlock specific profile
caam unlock --stale               # Clean all stale locks
caam unlock codex work-1 --force  # Force unlock even if process running
```

## Safety
- Default: Only unlock if process not running
- --force: Unlock even if process appears running (dangerous)
- Warn before force unlock
- Consider sending SIGTERM to process before unlocking

## Implementation
- Check if lock is stale (PID not running)
- If stale, just remove lock file
- If --force, warn and remove anyway
- If not stale and no --force, error

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can unlock stale locks
- Force requires explicit flag
- Warning before dangerous operations","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:53:39.864750655-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:34:16.981893529-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:34:16.981893529-05:00","close_reason":"Added caam profile unlock command with stale lock detection and --force flag","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-82c","depends_on_id":"caam-pxg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:53:39.879518279-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"caam-82c","depends_on_id":"caam-pb7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:53:39.880805974-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"caam-8y9","title":"Implement CLI sync commands","description":"# Task: Implement CLI sync commands

## Context
Implement the CLI commands for managing the sync pool and triggering synchronization.

## Commands

### `caam sync`
Sync now with all machines in pool.

```bash
caam sync [flags]

Flags:
  --machine NAME    Sync only with specific machine
  --provider PROV   Sync only specific provider
  --profile PROF    Sync only specific profile
  --dry-run         Show what would sync without doing it
  --force           Force sync even if recently synced
  --json            Output results as JSON
```

Output:
```
Syncing with 3 machines...

  work-laptop (192.168.1.100):
    ✓ claude/alice@gmail.com: pushed (local fresher)
    ✓ claude/bob@gmail.com: pulled (remote fresher)
    ✓ codex/work@company.com: up to date
    
  home-desktop (10.0.0.50):
    ✓ claude/alice@gmail.com: pushed
    ✓ gemini/personal@gmail.com: pulled
    
  cloud-vm (34.123.45.67):
    ✗ Connection failed: timeout

Sync complete: 4 pushed, 2 pulled, 1 up to date, 1 error
```

### `caam sync init` ⭐ NEW
Interactive first-time setup wizard:

```bash
caam sync init [flags]

Flags:
  --discover    Auto-discover from SSH config
  --csv         Create CSV template only
```

Flow:
```
$ caam sync init

Welcome to CAAM Sync Setup!

This will help you set up syncing between your machines.

Step 1: Create your sync pool

  Discovered hosts from ~/.ssh/config:
    [1] work-laptop (192.168.1.100)
    [2] home-desktop (10.0.0.50)
    [3] cloud-vm (34.123.45.67)
    [a] Add all discovered hosts
    [m] Manually add a machine
    [s] Skip for now

  Choice: a

Step 2: Test connectivity

  Testing work-laptop... ✓ OK (23ms)
  Testing home-desktop... ✓ OK (45ms)  
  Testing cloud-vm... ✗ Failed: connection refused

  1 machine failed. Continue anyway? (Y/n): y

Step 3: Enable auto-sync?

  Auto-sync automatically pushes fresh tokens when you backup or refresh.
  
  Enable auto-sync? (y/N): y

Setup complete!
  Machines in pool: 3 (2 online, 1 offline)
  Auto-sync: enabled

Run 'caam sync' to sync now, or it will happen automatically.
```

### `caam sync status`
Show sync pool status and last sync times.

```bash
caam sync status [flags]

Flags:
  --json    Output as JSON
```

Output:
```
Sync Status

Local Machine: jeffs-macbook
Auto-sync: enabled
Last full sync: 2025-12-19 14:29:00

Machines in pool:
  NAME           ADDRESS          STATUS    LAST SYNC
  work-laptop    192.168.1.100    🟢 online  5 min ago
  home-desktop   10.0.0.50        🟢 online  5 min ago
  cloud-vm       34.123.45.67     🔴 offline 2 hours ago (timeout)

Profiles tracked: 8
  claude: 5 profiles
  codex: 2 profiles
  gemini: 1 profile

Queue: 0 pending | History: 127 entries
```

### `caam sync add`
Add a machine to the sync pool.

```bash
caam sync add <name> <address> [flags]

Flags:
  --key PATH        Path to SSH private key (default: auto-detect)
  --user NAME       SSH username (default: current user)
  --remote-path P   Path to caam data on remote (default: same as local)
  --test            Test connectivity after adding
  --no-test         Skip connectivity test
```

Examples:
```bash
# Basic add (auto-detects SSH key)
caam sync add work-laptop 192.168.1.100

# With specific key and test
caam sync add cloud-vm 34.123.45.67 --key ~/.ssh/cloud_key --test

# With port and user in address
caam sync add dev-server jeff@dev.example.com:2222
```

### `caam sync remove`
Remove a machine from the sync pool.

```bash
caam sync remove <name> [flags]

Flags:
  --force    Skip confirmation
```

### `caam sync test`
Test connectivity to one or all machines.

```bash
caam sync test [name] [flags]

Flags:
  --all     Test all machines in pool
```

Output for single machine:
```
Testing connection to work-laptop (192.168.1.100)...
  SSH connection: ✓ OK (latency: 23ms)
  SFTP subsystem: ✓ OK
  CAAM data path: ✓ Found (~/.local/share/caam)
  CAAM version: v1.2.3 (compatible)
  Profiles found: 5
Connection successful!
```

Output for --all:
```
Testing all machines...
  work-laptop:   ✓ OK (23ms, 5 profiles)
  home-desktop:  ✓ OK (45ms, 3 profiles)
  cloud-vm:      ✗ Failed: connection refused

2/3 machines online
```

### `caam sync enable` / `caam sync disable`
Enable or disable auto-sync.

```bash
caam sync enable     # Enable auto-sync after backup/refresh
caam sync disable    # Disable auto-sync
```

### `caam sync log`
Show sync history.

```bash
caam sync log [flags]

Flags:
  --limit N     Show last N entries (default: 20)
  --machine M   Filter by machine
  --provider P  Filter by provider
  --errors      Show only errors
  --json        Output as JSON
```

Output:
```
Sync History (last 20 entries)

TIME                 MACHINE        PROFILE                  ACTION   STATUS
2025-12-19 14:29:00  work-laptop    claude/alice@gmail.com   push     ✓
2025-12-19 14:28:55  work-laptop    codex/work@company.com   pull     ✓
2025-12-19 14:28:50  home-desktop   claude/bob@gmail.com     push     ✓
2025-12-19 12:15:30  cloud-vm       *                        sync     ✗ timeout
...
```

### `caam sync discover`
Discover machines from SSH config.

```bash
caam sync discover [flags]

Flags:
  --add       Add discovered machines to pool
  --test      Test connectivity to discovered machines
```

Output:
```
Discovered machines from ~/.ssh/config:

  NAME           ADDRESS          KEY                   STATUS
  work-laptop    192.168.1.100    ~/.ssh/work_key      not in pool
  home-desktop   10.0.0.50        ~/.ssh/id_ed25519    not in pool
  github         github.com       (skipped: git host)

Add discovered machines to sync pool? (y/N): y

Added 2 machines. Run 'caam sync test --all' to verify connectivity.
```

### `caam sync edit`
Open sync config (CSV file) in editor.

```bash
caam sync edit
```

Opens `~/.caam/sync_machines.csv` in $EDITOR (or sensible default).

### `caam sync queue`
Manage the retry queue.

```bash
caam sync queue [flags]

Flags:
  --clear     Clear all pending retries
  --process   Process pending retries now
  --json      Output as JSON
```

Output:
```
Sync Queue: 2 pending

  PROFILE                  MACHINE       ATTEMPTS   LAST ERROR
  claude/alice@gmail.com   cloud-vm      2          connection refused
  codex/work@company.com   cloud-vm      1          timeout

Use 'caam sync queue --process' to retry now.
Use 'caam sync queue --clear' to clear the queue.
```

### Location
`cmd/caam/cmd/sync.go` (new file)

### Structure
```go
var syncCmd = &cobra.Command{
    Use:   "sync",
    Short: "Sync vault across machines",
    Long:  `Synchronize auth profiles with other machines in your sync pool.`,
    RunE:  runSync,
}

var syncInitCmd = &cobra.Command{...}     // First-time setup wizard
var syncStatusCmd = &cobra.Command{...}
var syncAddCmd = &cobra.Command{...}
var syncRemoveCmd = &cobra.Command{...}
var syncTestCmd = &cobra.Command{...}
var syncEnableCmd = &cobra.Command{...}
var syncDisableCmd = &cobra.Command{...}
var syncLogCmd = &cobra.Command{...}
var syncDiscoverCmd = &cobra.Command{...}
var syncEditCmd = &cobra.Command{...}
var syncQueueCmd = &cobra.Command{...}

func init() {
    rootCmd.AddCommand(syncCmd)
    syncCmd.AddCommand(syncInitCmd)
    syncCmd.AddCommand(syncStatusCmd)
    syncCmd.AddCommand(syncAddCmd)
    // etc...
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `caam sync` syncs with all machines
- [ ] `caam sync --machine` syncs with specific machine
- [ ] `caam sync --dry-run` shows preview
- [ ] `caam sync init` runs first-time setup wizard
- [ ] `caam sync status` shows pool status
- [ ] `caam sync add` adds machine to pool
- [ ] `caam sync remove` removes machine from pool
- [ ] `caam sync test` tests connectivity (single and all)
- [ ] `caam sync enable/disable` toggles auto-sync
- [ ] `caam sync log` shows history with filters
- [ ] `caam sync discover` finds machines from SSH config
- [ ] `caam sync edit` opens CSV in editor
- [ ] `caam sync queue` manages retry queue
- [ ] All commands have help text
- [ ] --json flag works for scriptability
- [ ] Error messages are helpful
- [ ] Works gracefully when no machines configured

- `cmd/caam/cmd/sync_test.go`

 {"id":"caam-fwz","title":"OAuth refresh handler for Claude Code","description":"# OAuth Refresh Handler for Claude Code

## Purpose
Implement OAuth token refresh for Claude Code to automatically renew tokens before expiry.

## Research Required
**IMPORTANT**: Claude Code OAuth implementation needs reverse engineering.

### Questions to Answer
1. Where is the oauth.json file located?
2. What is the token endpoint URL?
3. What is the client_id?
4. Is it JSON body or form-encoded?
5. What scopes are required?

### Likely Locations to Check
- `~/.claude/oauth.json`
- `~/.claude.ai/oauth.json`  
- `~/.config/claude/oauth.json`
- Check Claude Code source/docs if available

## Technical Requirements (Template)

### Refresh Function
```go
func RefreshClaudeToken(ctx context.Context, refreshToken string) (*TokenResponse, error) {
    // Build request
    body := map[string]string{
        "client_id":     claudeClientID,
        "grant_type":    "refresh_token",
        "refresh_token": refreshToken,
        // "scope":      "...",  // if needed
    }
    
    req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, "POST", claudeTokenURL, ...)
    req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")  // or form-encoded
    
    // Execute
    resp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("refresh request failed: %w", err)
    }
    defer resp.Body.Close()
    
    // Parse response
    if resp.StatusCode != 200 {
        // Handle error
    }
    
    var tokenResp TokenResponse
    json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&tokenResp)
    
    return &tokenResp, nil
}
```

### TokenResponse Structure
```go
type TokenResponse struct {
    AccessToken  string `json:"access_token"`
    RefreshToken string `json:"refresh_token,omitempty"` // May rotate
    ExpiresIn    int    `json:"expires_in"`              // Seconds
    ExpiresAt    string `json:"expires_at,omitempty"`    // ISO8601
    TokenType    string `json:"token_type"`
}
```

### File Update
```go
func UpdateClaudeAuth(path string, resp *TokenResponse) error {
    // Read existing file
    existing, _ := os.ReadFile(path)
    var auth map[string]any
    json.Unmarshal(existing, &auth)
    
    // Update fields
    auth["access_token"] = resp.AccessToken
    if resp.RefreshToken != "" {
        auth["refresh_token"] = resp.RefreshToken
    }
    auth["expires_at"] = resp.ExpiresAt
    // ... or calculate from expires_in
    
    // Atomic write
    return atomicWriteJSON(path, auth)
}
```

## Safety Requirements
1. **Never lose refresh token** - if refresh fails, keep original
2. **Atomic writes** - use temp file + rename
3. **Backup original** - keep .bak file before overwrite
4. **Verify new token** - optionally test before committing

## Error Handling
- Invalid refresh token → mark profile as "needs login"
- Network error → retry with backoff
- Rate limited → respect Retry-After header
- Unknown error → log and keep original token

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Research: Document Claude Code OAuth endpoints

- [ ] UpdateClaudeAuth with atomic writes
- [ ] Unit tests with mock HTTP server
- [ ] Integration test with real credentials (optional, manual)
- [ ] Error handling for all failure modes

## Files to Create/Modify
- `internal/refresh/claude.go` (new)
- `internal/refresh/claude_test.go` (new)

- Token expiry parsing (for reading current state)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:20:23.871008574-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T17:35:02.534599402-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T17:35:02.534599402-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented OAuth refresh logic for Claude Code.  {"id":"caam-gir","title":"Runtime Enhancements","description":"# Runtime Enhancements

## Purpose
Improve runtime behavior with hot reload, file watching, and signal handling for a smoother user experience.

## Background (from codex-pool)
codex-pool supports hot reload without restart:
```go
POST /admin/reload → h.pool.replace(newAccounts)
```

## Why This Matters
Users often:
1. Run TUI in one terminal
2. Add/modify profiles in another terminal
3. Expect TUI to reflect changes

Without hot reload, users must restart TUI to see changes.

## What This Epic Delivers
1. File watching for profiles directory (fsnotify)
2. Automatic TUI refresh on changes
3. `caam profiles reload` CLI command
4. SIGHUP handling for daemon mode
5. Graceful reload (no state loss)

## Technical Approach

### File Watching
Use fsnotify to watch:
- `~/.caam/profiles/` directory
- Provider-specific auth directories (optional)

Events to handle:
- CREATE: New profile added
- MODIFY: Profile updated
- DELETE: Profile removed
- RENAME: Profile renamed

### TUI Integration
```go
func (m *Model) watchProfiles() {
    watcher, _ := fsnotify.NewWatcher()
    watcher.Add(profilesDir)
    
    for event := range watcher.Events {
        m.reloadProfiles()  // async refresh
    }
}
```

### Signal Handling
```go
sigChan := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
signal.Notify(sigChan, syscall.SIGHUP)

go func() {
    for range sigChan {
        reloadConfiguration()
    }
}()
```

## User Experience
```
# Terminal 1: TUI running
┌─ Claude Profiles ─────────┐
│ ● work@company.com   🟢   │
│   personal@gmail.com 🟡   │
└───────────────────────────┘

# Terminal 2: Add new profile
$ caam backup claude newproject@corp.com
Profile saved.

# Terminal 1: Auto-updates!
┌─ Claude Profiles ─────────┐
│ ● work@company.com   🟢   │
│   personal@gmail.com 🟡   │
│   newproject@corp.com 🟢  │  ← NEW
└───────────────────────────┘
```

## Edge Cases
- Rapid file changes: Debounce (100ms)
- Corrupted profile: Log warning, skip
- Permission denied: Log error, continue watching
- Watch limit exceeded: Fall back to polling

 {"id":"caam-hzu","title":"Add AuthModeDeviceCode to provider interface","description":"# Add AuthModeDeviceCode to Provider Interface

## What

Extend the provider.AuthMode enum to include device code flow as a first-class authentication method.

## Why

The OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant (RFC 8628) is fundamentally different from browser-based OAuth:
- No localhost redirect needed
- User authenticates on a separate device
- CLI polls for token completion
- Better UX for headless environments

This deserves its own AuthMode rather than being a flag on OAuth because:
1. Different flow mechanics (polling vs redirect)
2. Different timeout characteristics
3. Different user interaction model
4. Profiles may be configured specifically for device-code-only scenarios

## Implementation Details

### File: internal/provider/provider.go

Add new constant:
\`\`\`go
const (
    AuthModeOAuth      AuthMode = "oauth"       // Browser-based OAuth flow
    AuthModeAPIKey     AuthMode = "api-key"     // API key authentication
    AuthModeDeviceCode AuthMode = "device-code" // OAuth device code flow (RFC 8628)
    AuthModeVertexADC  AuthMode = "vertex-adc"  // Vertex AI ADC
)
\`\`\`

### Interface Extension

Consider adding optional interface for providers that support device code:
\`\`\`go
// DeviceCodeProvider extends Provider with device code flow support
type DeviceCodeProvider interface {
    Provider
    // SupportsDeviceCode returns true if this provider supports device code flow
    SupportsDeviceCode() bool
    // LoginWithDeviceCode initiates device code authentication
    LoginWithDeviceCode(ctx context.Context, p *profile.Profile) error
}
\`\`\`

- [ ] AuthModeDeviceCode constant added
- [ ] DeviceCodeProvider interface defined (optional extension)
- [ ] Existing code continues to work (no breaking changes)
- [ ] Documentation comments explain when to use device-code vs oauth

## Testing

- Unit test that AuthModeDeviceCode is distinct from other modes
- Verify SupportedAuthModes() can include multiple modes

- Keep backward compatible - existing profiles with "oauth" continue to work

 {"id":"caam-zha","title":"Profile export/import for cross-machine transfer","description":"# Profile Export/Import for Cross-Machine Transfer

## What

Add \`caam export\` and \`caam import\` commands to easily transfer profiles between machines.

## Why - This is the UNIVERSAL headless solution

The most caam-native way to set up headless servers:

1. Login on machine with browser (laptop, workstation)
2. \`caam export codex/work > profile.tar.gz\`
3. \`scp profile.tar.gz user@server:~\`
4. On server: \`caam import profile.tar.gz\`
5. \`caam activate codex work\` - instant, no login needed!

This works for **ALL providers** (Codex, Claude, Gemini) because it transfers the auth FILES, not worrying about login mechanisms.

## Commands

### caam export

\`\`\`bash
# Export single profile
caam export codex/work > codex-work.tar.gz
caam export codex work -o codex-work.tar.gz

# Export all profiles for a tool
caam export codex --all > codex-all.tar.gz

# Export entire vault
caam export --all > full-vault.tar.gz
\`\`\`

### caam import

\`\`\`bash
# Import from file
caam import codex-work.tar.gz

# Import from stdin (for piping)
cat codex-work.tar.gz | caam import -

# Import with rename
caam import codex-work.tar.gz --as codex/server-work
\`\`\`

## Implementation

### Export format

Tar.gz containing:
- Profile directory structure from vault
- Metadata file with export timestamp, source machine, caam version
- Checksum for integrity

### File: cmd/caam/cmd/export.go

\`\`\`go
var exportCmd = &cobra.Command{
    Use:   "export <tool/profile>",
    Short: "Export profile(s) for transfer to another machine",
    Long: \`Export profile auth files for transfer to another machine.

This is the recommended way to set up profiles on headless servers:
1. Login normally on a machine with a browser
2. Export the profile: caam export codex/work > profile.tar.gz
3. Transfer to headless server: scp profile.tar.gz server:
4. Import on server: caam import profile.tar.gz
5. Activate: caam activate codex work

The exported file contains only the auth credentials, not session state.\`,
    RunE: runExport,
}
\`\`\`

## Security Considerations

- Exported files contain sensitive OAuth tokens
- Recommend encrypting with gpg for transfer: \`caam export ... | gpg -c > profile.tar.gz.gpg\`
- Add --encrypt flag for built-in encryption (nice-to-have)
- Document that exported tokens should be treated like passwords

- [ ] \`caam import profile.tar.gz\` restores profile correctly
- [ ] Imported profile can be activated immediately
- [ ] Works for all providers (codex, claude, gemini)

 {"id":"caam-zti","title":"Smart auto-backup before switch","description":"Prevent accidental data loss when switching profiles.

## The Problem
User modifies their auth (e.g., logs into new account in browser), then runs `caam activate foo`. Their unsaved changes are lost because the current state doesn't match any vault profile.

## Solution
Before restoring a profile, check if current auth state is 'unsaved':
1. Hash current auth files
2. Compare with all vault profiles
3. If NO MATCH → current state would be lost
4. Auto-backup to `_backup_<YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS>` profile
5. Continue with normal activate

## Config Option
```yaml
safety:
  auto_backup_before_switch: smart  # 'always' | 'smart' | 'never'
  max_auto_backups: 5  # Keep last N auto-backups, delete older
```

- 'smart': Only backup if state doesn't match any profile (default)
- 'always': Always backup before switch (creates many profiles)
- 'never': Skip (for power users who know what they're doing)

## Auto-backup Rotation
When max_auto_backups exceeded:
1. List all _backup_* profiles for the tool
2. Sort by timestamp (oldest first)
3. Delete oldest until count <= max

## Implementation
1. Add Vault.CurrentStateMatchesAny(fileSet) method
2. Add Vault.BackupCurrent(fileSet) -> '_backup_<timestamp>'
3. Add auto-backup rotation logic
4. Modify activate command to check config and call BackupCurrent
5. Print: 'Auto-backed up current state to _backup_20251217_143022'

- cmd/caam/cmd/activate.go: Add smart backup logic

-{"id":"caam-zxk","title":"Implement auto-sync triggering after auth changes","description":"# Task: Implement auto-sync triggering after auth changes

## Context
When auth is refreshed or backed up locally, automatically push the fresh token to all machines in the sync pool. This is the "push-based, not polling" requirement.

**IMPORTANT**: Auto-sync is OPT-IN. Users must explicitly enable it with `caam sync enable`.

## Trigger Points

### After Backup
When `caam backup <provider> <profile>` completes successfully:
1. Check if auto-sync is enabled
2. If yes, trigger sync for that specific profile

### After Token Refresh
When token is refreshed (via `caam refresh` or proactive refresh):
1. Check if auto-sync is enabled
2. If yes, trigger sync for that specific profile

### After Activate (when token changes)
When `caam activate` results in a token update:
1. Check if auto-sync is enabled
2. If yes, trigger sync

## Implementation

### Sync Hook Function
```go
package sync

// TriggerSyncIfEnabled checks if auto-sync is enabled and triggers sync
// Returns immediately - sync runs in background
func TriggerSyncIfEnabled(provider, profile string) {
    state, err := LoadSyncState()
    if err != nil {
        return // Sync not configured, silently ignore
    }
    
    // CRITICAL: Both must be enabled
    if !state.Pool.Enabled || !state.Pool.AutoSync {
        return // Sync disabled, do nothing
    }
    
    if len(state.Pool.Machines) == 0 {
        return // No machines to sync with
    }
    
    // Check throttle to prevent sync storms
    if !state.Throttler.ShouldSync(provider, profile) {
        return // Recently synced, skip
    }
    
    // Run sync in background goroutine
    go func() {
        ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Minute)
        defer cancel()
        
        syncer := NewSyncer(state)
        results, err := syncer.SyncProfile(ctx, provider, profile)
        
        // Log results
        logSyncResults(results, err)
        
        // Queue failures for retry
        if err != nil || hasFailures(results) {
            state.Queue.Add(provider, profile, getFailedMachines(results))
            state.Save()
        }
        
        // Update throttle timestamp
        state.Throttler.RecordSync(provider, profile)
    }()
}
```

### Sync Queue for Retries
When sync fails (machine offline, network error), queue for retry:

```go
type SyncQueue struct {
    Entries []QueueEntry `json:"entries"`
    MaxSize int          `json:"max_size"` // Default: 100
}

type QueueEntry struct {
    Provider    string    `json:"provider"`
    Profile     string    `json:"profile"`
    Machine     string    `json:"machine"`
    AddedAt     time.Time `json:"added_at"`
    Attempts    int       `json:"attempts"`
    LastAttempt time.Time `json:"last_attempt"`
    LastError   string    `json:"last_error"`
}

func (q *SyncQueue) Add(provider, profile string, failedMachines []string)
func (q *SyncQueue) ProcessPending(ctx context.Context) ([]*SyncResult, error)
func (q *SyncQueue) Clear()
func (q *SyncQueue) RemoveOld(maxAge time.Duration)
```

### Queue Processing
Process queue on `caam sync` or periodically during other operations:
```go
func processQueueIfNeeded(ctx context.Context) {
    state, err := LoadSyncState()
    if err != nil || !state.Pool.Enabled {
        return
    }
    
    // Clean up old entries (> 24 hours)
    state.Queue.RemoveOld(24 * time.Hour)
    
    if len(state.Queue.Entries) == 0 {
        return
    }
    
    // Process queue in background
    go func() {
        results, _ := state.Queue.ProcessPending(ctx)
        logSyncResults(results, nil)
    }()
}
```

### Rate Limiting (Throttling)
Prevent sync storms if many changes happen quickly:
```go
type SyncThrottler struct {
    lastSync    map[string]time.Time // key: "provider/profile"
    minInterval time.Duration        // Default: 30 seconds
    mu          sync.RWMutex
}

func NewThrottler(minInterval time.Duration) *SyncThrottler

func (t *SyncThrottler) ShouldSync(provider, profile string) bool {
    t.mu.RLock()
    defer t.mu.RUnlock()
    
    key := provider + "/" + profile
    if last, ok := t.lastSync[key]; ok {
        if time.Since(last) < t.minInterval {
            return false // Too soon, skip
        }
    }
    return true
}

func (t *SyncThrottler) RecordSync(provider, profile string) {
    t.mu.Lock()
    defer t.mu.Unlock()
    t.lastSync[provider+"/"+profile] = time.Now()
}
```

}

Process queue on `caam sync` or periodically during other operations:
```go
func processQueueIfNeeded(ctx context.Context) {
    state, err := LoadSyncState()
    if err != nil || !state.Pool.Enabled {
        return
    }
    
    // Clean up old entries (> 24 hours)
    state.Queue.RemoveOld(24 * time.Hour)
    
    if len(state.Queue.Entries) == 0 {
        return
    }
    
    // Process queue in background
    go func() {
        results, _ := state.Queue.ProcessPending(ctx)
        logSyncResults(results, nil)
    }()
}
```

### Rate Limiting (Throttling)
Prevent sync storms if many changes happen quickly:
```go
type SyncThrottler struct {
    lastSync    map[string]time.Time // key: "provider/profile"
    minInterval time.Duration        // Default: 30 seconds
    mu          sync.RWMutex
}

func NewThrottler(minInterval time.Duration) *SyncThrottler

func (t *SyncThrottler) ShouldSync(provider, profile string) bool {
    t.mu.RLock()
    defer t.mu.RUnlock()
    
    key := provider + "/" + profile
    if last, ok := t.lastSync[key]; ok {
        if time.Since(last) < t.minInterval {
            return false // Too soon, skip
        }
    }
    return true
}

func (t *SyncThrottler) RecordSync(provider, profile string) {
    t.mu.Lock()
    defer t.mu.Unlock()
    t.lastSync[provider+"/"+profile] = time.Now()
}
```

## Enabling Auto-Sync

### First Time Setup
```bash
$ caam sync enable

Auto-sync is now enabled. When you backup or refresh tokens,
they will automatically sync to machines in your sync pool.

Current sync pool: 0 machines
Add machines with: caam sync add <name> <address>

Tip: Use 'caam sync status' to check sync pool status.
```

### With Machines Already Added
```bash
$ caam sync enable

Auto-sync is now enabled.
Will sync with 3 machines: work-laptop, home-desktop, cloud-vm
```

### Disabling
```bash
$ caam sync disable

Auto-sync is now disabled.
Use 'caam sync' to manually sync when needed.
```

## Configuration
In `~/.caam/config.yaml`:
```yaml
sync:
  # Note: enabled and auto_sync are stored in sync state, not config
  # These are just defaults for new sync pools
  min_interval: 30s      # Minimum time between syncs for same profile
  retry_attempts: 3      # Max retry attempts for failed syncs
  retry_backoff: 5m      # Wait between retries
  queue_max_size: 100    # Max pending sync entries
  history_max_size: 1000 # Max history entries
```
